title: Vibrio Natriegens - Glycerol stock
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3nu1o","text":"Preparation of Vibrio natriegens cell stocks for long-term storage at -80C. Principle is to remove any accumulated metabolic waste from overnight culture and resuspend in 20% glycerol final.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6054g","text":"prepare 60% glycerol stock. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dn5kq","text":"prepare LB3 media (high salt media composed of LB added with 3% NaCl final).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Use a single colony to inoculate 1-3ml of LB3 media (add any required selection) and grow culture to density. Culture can be grown at 30C or 37C shaker at 250rpm.

Spin down 600uL of overnight culture at max speed for 1 min in room temp centrifuge. Remove the supernatant leaving the cell pellet intact.Resuspend the cell pellet with 400uL of growth media Transfer to cryotube.
Add 200uL of 60% glycerol to cryotube, to get to 20% glycerol final. Mix contents gently by rotating tube. Store tube at -80C
creator: Nili O
